title: drymocallis glandulosa var. campanulata
intro: Drymocallis glandulosa var. campanulata, also known as the western sweetroot, is a perennial plant native to the western regions of North America. This plant contains berberine, a compound with medicinal properties, particularly in treating gastrointestinal issues and infections. The health benefits of western sweetroot include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate digestive problems, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Western sweetroot can be prepared as a tincture, tea, or powder for use in herbal remedies.
